findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:207 frames the GPS dispute in grant-and-permit terms; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:217 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:221 argue the anti-disclosure case; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:273 explicitly defines deletion as courage rather than cowardice; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:301 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:305 execute the deletions after the scene has already morally ratified them; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:337 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:349 further sand down the report into a tidy protection logic.
findings.problem: The coordinate-deletion climax over-justifies itself on the page. Instead of leaving the choice ethically compromised, the scene repeatedly explains why deletion is the responsible, courageous answer and casts every institutional alternative as predatory. That moral safety valve reduces the ugliness of what they are actually doing: withholding precise data, planning to sandbag a report, and choosing opacity at the point of highest consequence. In this book's risk profile, that is sanitization drift.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:197, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:217, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:273, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:279, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:337 so the GPS argument stays morally double-edged. Keep the deletion, but cut or roughen the thesis-like justifications, add a concrete protection or scientific use they are sacrificing by deleting the mark, and let at least one character articulate the risk that this choice may also fail the creek later. Keep the exchange anchored to the live scene rather than letting it resolve into a clean statement of principle.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:197, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:279, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:337 leaves deletion ethically unresolved on the page: at least one speaker names a specific protection or knowledge they are losing by destroying the coordinate, no line explicitly labels the act as courage or responsibility, and the text immediately after the delete action still carries one concrete downside rather than thematic closure. FAIL if the scene still reads as a fully argued proof that deletion is simply the right answer.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:137 opens with "The basin had been hit hard."; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:241 opens with "The basin held them."; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:291 opens with "The basin seemed to wait with her."
findings.problem: The chapter repeats the same personified place-opening three times with only the verb changing. Once the basin reveal is underway, that exact sentence shape becomes a visible tic rather than a fresh choice, flattening a setting that should keep evolving under pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:241 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:291, or one of those lines plus snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:137, so at least one beat enters through new sensory data or Mina's body instead of another "The basin ..." opening. Keep the location active, but vary the construction rather than personifying the basin in the same slot three times.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if, after revision, no more than two of the three beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:137, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:241, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:291 use the exact "The basin ..." opening and at least one of them arrives through changed light, water, sound, or Mina's bodily response. FAIL if all three still reuse the same opening template.

summary: Chapter 17 lands the required vigil beats, preserves canonical continuity, and keeps Mina, Rhea, and Tomas broadly in voice, so the booker and dialogue lenses clear. It fails craft because the coordinate-deletion climax explains its ethics too cleanly and turns a compromised act into a near-ratified principle, and it fails prose because the repeated "The basin ..." opening becomes a noticeable sentence-shape tic at the scene's peak.
